title: Cymbalaria longipes

This family is also commonly known as the plantain family, due to the similarity in appearance of some of its members to the common plantain plant. Cymbalaria longipes is also closely related to other species within the genus Cymbalaria.

intro: Cymbalaria longipes, also known as the keel-flowered toadflax, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the family Plantaginaceae. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues, fever, and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of C. longipes include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal activities. In herbal medicine, C. longipes is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and it is also used in the form of tinctures, teas, and salves to treat various health conditions.
